commands.repair_reason: The PR needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof and maintainer confirmation of the preservation-only compatibility boundary before merge.; Cleared: The diff only changes cron persisted-shape validation and does not touch dependencies, CI, package scripts, credentials, permissions, or supply-chain surfaces. commands.review_summary:
This PR changes `getInvalidPersistedCronJobReason` so persisted cron payloads with any non-empty string `payload.kind` pass validation while known `systemEvent` and `agentTurn` shapes keep their structural checks.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: seed `jobs.json` with an unsupported string `payload.kind`, then the current validator marks it invalid and the load path omits it from `state.store.jobs`. I did not run a live reload because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: The PR needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof and maintainer confirmation of the preservation-only compatibility boundary before merge.

target.title: fix(cron): accept unknown payload.kind in persisted store validation
target.body:
## Summary

- `getInvalidPersistedCronJobReason` in `src/cron/persisted-shape.ts` rejected any `payload.kind` that was not exactly `"systemEvent"` or `"agentTurn"`, silently dropping cron jobs with legacy kinds (e.g. `"command"`, `"agentmessage"`) from the loaded set on every store reload cycle.
- Relax the validation to only reject missing or non-string `payload.kind` values. Known kinds (`systemEvent`, `agentTurn`) retain their structural validation; unknown kinds pass through so that `doctor-cron-store-migration` can normalize them.
- What did NOT change: schedule validation, payload object presence check, `systemEvent` text validation, `agentTurn` message validation, store reload cycle, sessionTarget defaulting logic.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security
- [ ] Chore

## Scope

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Cron jobs with legacy `payload.kind` values (e.g. `"command"`, `"agentmessage"`) were silently dropped from the loaded set because the persisted store validation only accepted `"systemEvent"` or `"agentTurn"`. Three production cron jobs were lost for 12 days before discovery.

**Real environment tested:** Node 22.x on Linux, `node --import tsx` against the actual `getInvalidPersistedCronJobReason` function from source.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:** Ran `node --import tsx` calling `getInvalidPersistedCronJobReason` from `src/cron/persisted-shape.ts` with 9 test cases covering known valid, legacy, and invalid payload kinds.

**Evidence after fix:** Console output from `node --import tsx` running the actual production function:

    Testing getInvalidPersistedCronJobReason from src/cron/persisted-shape.ts:

      systemEvent valid: ACCEPTED
      agentTurn valid: ACCEPTED
      command (legacy, should pass): ACCEPTED
      agentmessage (legacy, should pass): ACCEPTED
      missing kind (should reject): REJECTED (invalid-payload)
      empty kind (should reject): REJECTED (invalid-payload)
      numeric kind (should reject): REJECTED (invalid-payload)
      systemEvent missing text (should reject): REJECTED (invalid-payload)
      agentTurn empty message (should reject): REJECTED (invalid-payload)

    Legacy payload.kind values (command, agentmessage) now pass validation.
    Known kinds (systemEvent, agentTurn) retain structural validation.

**Observed result after the fix:** Legacy payload kinds `"command"` and `"agentmessage"` are now accepted. Known kinds retain structural validation (missing `text` or empty `message` still rejected). Invalid payload kinds (missing, empty, non-string) still rejected.

**What was not tested:** Live cron store reload with a persisted `command`-kind job file; `doctor --fix` migration of legacy kinds to canonical forms.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `getInvalidPersistedCronJobReason` used a strict whitelist `payloadKind !== "systemEvent" && payloadKind !== "agentTurn"` that rejected all legacy/custom payload kinds.
- Missing detection: no allowlist extensibility or passthrough for unknown-but-valid kinds; the validation was written assuming only two kinds would ever exist.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: unit test for `getInvalidPersistedCronJobReason`
- Scenario: legacy kinds (`command`, `agentmessage`) should return `null` (valid); invalid kinds (missing, empty, numeric) should return `"invalid-payload"`; known kinds retain structural checks.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it locks the relaxed validation contract without requiring a live cron store.

Closes #84922
